goals.primary: Stay healthy to the start line

zones.threshold: 155-164 (not used this cycle - no dedicated threshold sessions; MP 8:30 sits well below this zone)

tibRules: Flare trigger is SUSTAINED fast pace in the 7:30-7:45 band, not volume or consecutive days - with no work scheduled faster than marathon pace (8:30) anywhere in this plan, that band is never approached.
Nothing sustained faster than 8:30/mi anywhere in the plan. Marathon pace 8:30 is the safe race-specific workhorse - there is no dedicated threshold work this cycle.
Fast bursts (strides, <=90s reps) are safe but never sprint-fast - the only faster-than-MP stimulus in the block.
Marathon pace 8:30 is the safe, goal-pace workhorse.
Long-run pace-up segments are capped at MP (8:30), built gradually within an easy base, and dropped immediately if the knee or tib speaks.
Max one MP-focused bout per week, living mostly in the long run.
Morning-after check (touch-tenderness + stairs, for both knee and tib) governs progression, not in-run feel.

structure.Base: Weeks 1-5 (Jul 6 - Aug 9): 15.5->22 mpw, easy aerobic + strides (knee return protocol wks 1-2), LR 8->10 (no weekend long wk1 - travel/knee)
structure.Build: Weeks 6-11 (Aug 10 - Sep 20): 24->30 mpw (dips to 18 in travel-cutback wk9), easy aerobic + strides, MP (8:30) touches enter gradually within the long run, LR 12->14, no threshold work
structure.Peak: Weeks 12-14 (Sep 21 - Oct 11): 33->40 mpw, LR to 22 on Oct 10, one MP-focused (8:30) long run per week, no threshold
structure.Taper: Weeks 15-17 (Oct 12 - Nov 1): volume drops to 30/24, then race week (~38 incl. race), short MP (8:30) touches to stay sharp, race Nov 1

days.focus: Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.

Settle into the block. Truly easy, HR under 145. You're starting from real fitness (~17 mpw last week), so no need to force anything. First of three easy days in a row this week - keep it comfortably controlled.

Highest-mileage day this week. Easy run, then 4x ~20s relaxed strides at the end on flat ground. Strides are quick but NOT sprints - smooth turnover, well short of your flare pace. Full walk recovery between.

Third easy day in a row (5/6.5/4). Keep it genuinely easy - this is the biggest consecutive-day block you'll have done, but all easy pace so it's leg fatigue, not tib risk. Morning-after check tomorrow matters.
Rest (your preferred rest day this week). You've run three in a row Tue-Thu; this sets up the weekend. Tib check.
Rest - this week's short easy + leg-strength session moved to Sunday. Tib check.
Full rest (knee). Structural, not conditional. Morning knee/tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs. Rolls into week 2's return-from-knee protocol.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Full rest (knee). Morning knee/tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.

First run back, conditional on a calm knee - no swelling, pain-free stairs. If either is off, delay to Thursday or start with a 2-mi test and stop if anything grumbles. Genuinely easy pace, no strides.
Easy aerobic. No strides yet this week - keep it plain while the knee settles back into running.
Rest before the long run. Held regardless of how the week felt.

Air quality is too high to run outside, so this moves to the treadmill and splits into two easy blocks: 5 mi, then 3 mi, both today. Together they are the 8-mile easy long run; keep both at the same easy effort with no pace-up. Log each block to Strava as its own run; the app now sums both same-day runs into today's total. Stop early if the knee speaks.

segments.note: First block. Easy effort, settle in and get the legs moving.
Second block, finishes today's 8. Same easy effort - no pace-up to make up for the treadmill.

days.focus: Light leg strength - calf raises, tibialis raises, gentle single-leg work. Skip loaded single-leg work if the knee is tender; posterior chain is fine either way.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy aerobic. No threshold work this cycle - everything stays comfortably easy.
Short easy shakeout - travel day, so keep it minimal and plain, no strides. The half-mile trimmed here goes into Saturday's longer run.
Easy aerobic.
Rest before the long run.

8.5 mi easy, first step up the ladder from last week's 8. All easy, no pace-up. Conditional on the 8-miler and its morning-afters coming back clean - if the knee left any tenderness, hold this at 8. Fuel every 30-40 min, HR under 150.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy.
Easy with 5x20s strides. Controlled speed.
Easy aerobic.
Rest before the long run.
7.5 mi easy, then 1 mi @ 8:30 (MP), then 1 mi easy. First taste of goal pace on a long run - controlled, not hard. Drop the MP mile to easy if the knee or tib speaks.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy aerobic.
Easy with 5x20s strides. Controlled speed, never sprint-fast.
Easy aerobic.
Rest before the long run.
7.5 mi easy, then 2 mi @ 8:30 (MP), then 1 mi easy. Building MP volume within the long run, controlled effort. Drop the MP segment to easy if the knee or tib speaks.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy. Build phase - volume climbs but keep easy days honestly easy.
Easy with strides.
Easy aerobic.
Rest before the long run.

8.5 mi easy, then 2 mi @ 8:30 (MP), then 2 mi easy. Bigger step up (from 10.5), so this one is conditional on clean morning-afters off last week's long run - if anything lingered, hold this at 11.5 instead. Time at goal pace on tiring legs, not a hammer session. Drop the MP segment to easy if the knee or tib speaks.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy aerobic. No threshold work this cycle.
Easy with 5x20s strides. Controlled speed.
Rest before tomorrow's moved-up long run. The shakeout is dropped so there's a genuine rest day ahead of the Friday long (that miles went to Tue/Wed). Tib check.

Long run pulled to Friday because of Sat-Sun travel. Easy effort, HR under 150, no MP segment this week. Sits between last week's 12 and next week's 13, so it fits the ramp. Fuel well. Weekend is travel/off.
Travel day. Rest.
Travel day. Rest — no leg strength this week given the moved long run and travel.

Exception to the usual Monday rest: the run that was on Tuesday moves here to break up the post-travel stretch (Fri long, then two travel-rest days). Easy pace off the travel. Tuesday is rest to compensate.
Rest to compensate for the Monday run. Tib check.
Easy with 6 strides.
Easy aerobic.
Rest before the long run.

9 mi easy, then 3 mi @ 8:30 (MP), then 2 mi easy. Longest MP block yet, one mile added to the easy warm-up to start the ladder toward a 20-miler in a few weeks. Drop the MP segment to easy if the knee or tib speaks.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. This whole week is a forced cutback from travel - treat it as a genuine recovery/down week. Well-timed at the block midpoint.
Easy aerobic. Enjoy the lighter week.
No run today (travel constraint). Rest.
Lighter easy run - short and gentle, travel week. No pressure on pace.
Another short, light easy run. Keep it truly relaxed.
Travel-shortened long - only 7, easy-steady, no MP. You're banking recovery this week, not fitness. Finish feeling fresh.
No run today (travel). Rest - no leg strength this week; the cutback is doing the work.
Exception to the usual Monday rest: a very short, easy 2-mi shakeout (you OK'd this). Legs are fresh off the cutback — just loosening up. Tuesday is rest to compensate.
Rest today to compensate for the Monday shakeout. Tib check.
Easy with strides. Back into the swing after the down week.
Easy aerobic.
Rest before the long run.
9 mi easy, then 3 mi @ 8:30 (MP), then 2 mi easy. Historical danger window (post-travel re-ramp) - keep the re-entry controlled, drop the MP segment to easy if the knee or tib speaks.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy, moderate volume - the week's bigger midweek effort now sits Wednesday, giving more distance from Saturday's long run.
This week's longest midweek run, with strides. Placed Wednesday to maximize freshness heading into Saturday's sustainability check.
Short and light, taper day into Friday's rest ahead of Saturday's long run.
Rest before the tune-up. Hydrate, normal dinner, lay out kit.

4 mi easy, then 6 mi @ 8:30 (MP) continuous, then 6 mi easy. The MP block itself is unchanged from the original sustainability check - only the easy miles around it grew, to keep building toward the 20-miler. If 8:30 settles in comfortably with HR around 150, that validates the goal pace. Drop the MP block to easy if the knee or tib speaks.
Light leg strength or rest depending on how the tib feels after Saturday's validation run. Prioritize recovery.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy, moderate volume - the bigger midweek effort sits Wednesday this week.
This week's longest midweek run, with strides, placed Wednesday for maximum freshness heading into Saturday's 18-mile long run.
Light, taper day into Friday's rest ahead of the long run.
Rest before the long run.
10 mi easy, then 5 mi @ 8:30 (MP), then 3 mi easy. Big race-specific long run, growing toward the 20 next week. Drop the MP block to easy if the knee or tib speaks.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.

weeks.label: Peak volume · long easy (time on feet)

days.focus: Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy, moderate volume - the week's bigger midweek effort now sits Wednesday instead, giving more distance from Saturday's long run.
This week's longest midweek run. Placed Wednesday (4 days post-long-run, 3 days pre-long-run) to maximize freshness heading into Saturday's 20-miler. Still fully easy effort.
Short and light, taper day into Friday's rest ahead of the 20-mile long run.
Rest before the long run.

20 miles, fully easy - the new peak-before-peak, slotting between the 18 you'd built to and the 22 the following week. Time on feet on tired legs, HR under 150. Fuel every 30-40 min. Cap effort, not distance - if anything feels off, ease the pace, never push.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy, moderate volume - the bigger midweek effort sits Wednesday this week.
This week's longest midweek run, placed Wednesday for maximum freshness heading into Saturday's 22-miler, the peak long run of the block.
Light, taper day into Friday's rest ahead of the 22.
Rest before the biggest run of the block. Fuel and hydrate well today.

The peak long run, mostly easy. 15 mi easy, then 5 mi @ 8:30 (MP), then 2 mi easy. Holding MP late is the race skill you need. Drop the MP segment to easy if anything is off - finishing 22 easy is still the win. Full race fueling rehearsal.
Light leg strength or rest — prioritize recovery from the 22.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy. No dedicated quality session - MP work now lives in the long run only.
Easy. Legs should start feeling fresher as taper takes hold.
Easy with strides to keep turnover crisp.
Rest before the long run.
Reduced long run - taper means less volume. 12 mi easy, then 2 mi @ 8:30 (MP). HR under 150 on the easy portion. Drop the MP segment if anything feels off.
Leg strength. Calf raises, tibialis raises, single-leg stability, posterior chain. Directly supports the anterior tib.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy with strides. Stay loose, resist adding work - the fitness is banked.
Easy. No dedicated quality session - MP work now lives in the long run only.
Short easy.
Rest before the last longer run.
Last moderately-long run. 9 mi easy, then 2 mi @ 8:30 (MP). Keep it comfortable - the work is done, this is just maintaining rhythm.
Light leg strength only. Prioritize freshness for race week.
Full rest. Structural, not conditional. Morning tib check: touch-tenderness + first flight of stairs.
Easy with a few strides. Legs should feel springy and rested now.
Short easy. Nothing hard this week - just keep the blood moving.
Very short. Warm up easy, then 2-3 x 90s at MP (8:30) just to feel goal pace. Stop feeling fresh, not tired. Last real pace touch before the race.
Rest. Travel to the expo / pick up your bib. Stay off your feet as much as you can.
Optional tiny 2-mi shakeout to calm nerves, or full rest if you prefer. Lay out your kit, plan your fueling, hydrate. Early night.

RACE DAY. Goal 3:43 at 8:30/mi, HR 148-153. Start controlled - bank nothing, let the first 10K feel almost easy. Settle into 8:30, fuel every 30-40 min. The knee and tib have been managed all block; race pace is safely outside your flare zone, so run your race. Adjust to what the day gives: sub-3:50 B-goal, and you beat 3:59 comfortably. Trust the block.
